title: Bridge Essay: A neural basis for distinguishing imagination from reality.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Imaginal** (Phase 10) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Imaginal to mechanistic neuroscience through A, neural, basis, for, distinguishing.

**Aneural basis for distinguishing imagination from reality.** — Neuron. PMID: 40480215. DOI: 10.1016/j.neuron.2025.05.015. Humans are able to imagine scenarios that are decoupled from the current environment by internally activating perceptual representations. Although an efficient re-use of existing resources, it remains unknown how human observers classify perceptual signals as reflecting external reality, as opposed to internal simulation or imagination. Here, we show that judgments of reality are underpinned by the combined strength of sensory activity generated by either imagery or perception in the fusiform gy
